body:
Adds CSharpier formatting for generated and hand-written C# code, with CI coverage for format checks.

Commits in order add the CI job and the csharpier application to the generated client before the large format changes.

## Blocked by

- #25 
- #32

Closes #27

comment_data.body:
I don't see it using 4-space indent anywhere, can you point to a specific line? Csharpier respects whatever setting for indents we have in the `.editorconfig`, which is set to 2. There are only 4 configuration options for csharpier:
```yaml
printWidth: 100
useTabs: false
indentSize: 4
endOfLine: auto
```

It will use whatever's in `.editorconfig`, or the defaults above, if no `.csharpierrc` is provided.

comment_data.body: I will also say that the standard for C# is 4-spaces, no tabs. Microsoft conventions here tend to win out in the majority of C# projects, and 2 spaces looks about as alien in C# as using spaces instead of tabs in go would look. These are definitely just 2 spaces, however.

comment_data.body:
> why does this only exist in the new commit? were there functional changes made somewhere?
> 
> or is it because this repo hasn't been regenerated for a long time?

It's just because it hasn't been generated for 8 years, I think. #32 covers the necessary api changes to get the client built again for neovim 0.12.2. Those commits are included in this branch -- just think it'd be good to keep separate for review purposes. This branch just has these actually functional commits to get csharpier up and running:
[feat: apply csharpier format to generated nvim client e323362](https://github.com/neovim/nvim.net/pull/33/commits/e32336200d84dbb71f008caea490073ffa9ae62f)
[ci: automatically check file formatting in ci c798d60](https://github.com/neovim/nvim.net/pull/33/commits/c798d60caa72038d0f01a0274d5c2121f14377f3)

Everything after that is noise from applying the formatter.

comment_data.body:
> i suppose the old style wasn't better.
> 
> what csharpier config are we actually using? should we commit an explicit config, or is it like python "black" where it has no config ?
> 
> i don't see a config file in this PR

You _can_ configure just these things:

```yaml
printWidth: 100
useTabs: false
indentSize: 4
endOfLine: auto
```

But we already configure those in `.editorconfig`. It's a very opinionated formatter, a bit less in-your-face than `black`, though.
